description: Established institutions often named after their physical seat of power.
factions: Clergy
Ordained priests wielding spiritual authority over the faithful and interpreting divine will for the masses.
Chamber
Lawmakers convening in a formal hall to debate, legislate, and settle disputes with binding authority.
Tribunal
Stern judges weighing evidence and pronouncing verdicts, their rulings carrying the weight of absolute law.
Jury
Citizens chosen by lot and deliberating behind closed doors, holding a defendant's fate in their hands.
Citadel
A towering fortress dominating the city below, sheltering the ruling power behind walls of stone.
Academy
Students and masters pursuing knowledge within guarded halls, competing fiercely for rank and discovery.
University
A sprawling seat of learning drawing scholars from distant lands, trading knowledge and forging rivalries.
College
A focused school training students in a specialized discipline, shaping the next generation of experts.
School
Instructors passing down skills or doctrine to pupils, molding young minds according to tradition or innovation.
Seminary
Aspiring priests and scholars training under strict doctrine, preparing to serve a faith or arcane tradition.
Monastery
Monks living, praying, and laboring behind cloistered walls, seeking enlightenment through discipline and devotion.
Convent
Cloistered sisters devoting their lives to prayer and service, wielding quiet influence from behind the veil.
Temple
The faithful gathering beneath sacred arches to worship, tithe, and seek divine favor.
Shrine
A hallowed site where pilgrims kneel, leaving offerings and prayers in hopes of a miracle.
Sanctuary
Consecrated walls where the desperate seek shelter and even kings hesitate to draw a sword.
Cathedral
A grand seat of worship towering over the city, housing the highest clergy and holiest relics.
Asylum
A refuge taking in the broken and the hunted, offering shelter that the outside world refuses.
Refuge
A hidden shelter harboring those fleeing danger, surviving on secrecy and the goodwill of protectors.
Haven
A place of safety drawing the weary and the hunted, promising peace behind its guarded threshold.
Sanctum
A warded inner chamber holding power or secrets so precious that only the chosen may enter.
Retreat
A secluded haven offering solitude for meditation, recovery, or planning -- far from the world's noise.
Armory
Racks of weapons and armor standing ready behind locked doors, fueling whatever force controls the key.
Forge
Smiths hammering glowing metal into weapons and tools, their craft essential to war and commerce alike.
Foundry
Molten metal pouring into molds day and night, producing the arms and fittings a realm demands.
Workshop
Artisans building, repairing, and inventing at crowded benches, filling the air with sparks and ambition.
Religion
An organized faith commanding millions through doctrine, ritual, and a hierarchy stretching from village shrine to holy seat.
Abbey
A sprawling religious estate governed by an abbot or abbess, balancing piety with the politics of land and wealth.
Religious Sect
Breakaway believers following controversial doctrines, drawing suspicion from the mainstream faith they once belonged to.
Conservatory
Talented students honing artistic mastery under demanding mentors, competing for recognition in music or the fine arts.
Hold
A fortified seat of power anchoring a faction's territory, serving as stronghold, armory, and command center.
Chantry
Priests or mages studying and practicing within consecrated walls, blending devotion with arcane or divine research.
Establishment
An entrenched institution shaping daily life through long-standing authority that few dare to question openly.
District
A defined quarter of a city developing its own character, loyalties, and unwritten rules.
